inputs: Raw video file (300-500 MB, MP4/MKV/MOV or similar)
properties.name: Upload Meeting Video
properties.description: User selects and uploads a meeting video file (300-500 MB) via the web interface. System validates the file, stores it in object storage, and enqueues an async transcription job.
properties.trigger: User initiates an upload action in the UI

outputs: Verbatim speaker-attributed transcript with timestamps stored in the system
Processing status updated to transcript ready
properties.assumption_note: Audio extraction step (demux from video container) is treated as an internal sub-step, not a separate business process, because it has no business decision point.
inputs: Speaker diarization request to ASR engine (EXT-02)
properties.name: Transcribe Audio (ASR + Diarization)
properties.description: System retrieves the uploaded video from storage, extracts audio, submits it to the ASR/diarization engine (EXT-02), receives speaker-attributed transcript segments, assembles and persists the verbatim transcript.
properties.trigger: Transcription job is dequeued from the async processing queue

properties.assumption_note: Simple retrieval/download operation. No complex branching at MVP scope.
properties.name: Download / Retrieve Transcript
properties.description: User views and downloads the completed verbatim transcript from the system. The transcript is rendered in the UI and offered as a downloadable text/document file.
properties.trigger: User navigates to transcript view and requests download

outputs: Processing status updated to protocol ready
properties.assumption_note: Protocol generation is triggered automatically after transcription completes; no manual user action is required to initiate it.
inputs: Prompt template for structured protocol extraction
properties.name: Generate Meeting Protocol
properties.description: System submits the verbatim transcript to the LLM protocol generator (EXT-03) with a structured extraction prompt. The LLM returns a meeting protocol. System persists the protocol as Markdown.
properties.trigger: Transcription job completes successfully (transcript is ready)

outputs: Updated meeting protocol saved as Markdown
properties.assumption_note: Free-form Markdown edit; no structured field validation required at MVP scope.
inputs: Generated meeting protocol in Markdown (from BP-004 or previous edit)
properties.name: Review and Edit Protocol
properties.description: User opens the generated protocol in the in-app Markdown editor, reviews its content, makes corrections or additions, and saves the updated version.
properties.trigger: User opens the protocol view and chooses to edit

properties.assumption_note: PDF rendering uses a server-side export script (e.g. Pandoc or headless browser). Specific tool TBD with tech stack.
inputs: Current meeting protocol Markdown (from BP-004 or BP-005)
properties.name: Export Protocol to PDF
properties.description: User requests a PDF export of the meeting protocol. System renders the Markdown to PDF via an export script and delivers the file as a download.
properties.trigger: User clicks Export to PDF in the protocol view
